source_quote: In the fourth quarter of 2015, Robert H. Eder (the Company’s Chairman and Chief Executive Officer) and Frank K. Rogers (the Company’s Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer) met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity.

additional_note: GHF (Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC) retained as financial advisor; became BMO Capital Markets Corp. on August 1, 2016

source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site.

source_quote: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers.

additional_note: IOI submitted after Party C joined the process late; Party C had management presentation by telephone on July 14, 2016
comments_2: Filing calls this an 'IOI' (indication of interest); per §G1 'non-binding indication of interest' is an informal trigger. Party C was a late joiner submitting before the LOI deadline.
comments_3: NA
source_quote: On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million). Subsequently, Party C was provided access to the internet data site and the Company’s management held a management presentation with Party C by telephone on July 14, 2016.

source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.

additional_note: Declined to shorten 30-day diligence period; Company declined to commit not to enter agreement with another bidder; Party D indicated would not proceed
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.

additional_note: Withdrew revised $23.81 proposal; confirmed original $21.26 proposal still stood; effectively withdrawn from active contention
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share)

additional_note: Final LOI $25.00/share all-cash (no CVR); submitted with merger agreement and voting agreement markups; G&W completed physical diligence August 10-11
comments_2: Still described as a 'revised LOI' (non-binding) per §G1 informal trigger. Although submitted with merger agreement markups, the filing does not describe it as a binding bid or financing commitment. Board still needed to evaluate and negotiate final agreement.
comments_3: NA
source_quote: G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W’s LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.

additional_note: Party B declined to raise its $24.00/share offer in response to G&W's $25.00 bid
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.

additional_note: Merger agreement signed August 12, 2016; $25.00/share all-cash; Pullman Acquisition Sub Inc. is merger sub; Company, G&W, and Eder Trusts also executed voting agreement
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement.

additional_note: Press release issued Monday August 15, 2016 prior to opening of NASDAQ trading

source_quote: The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.
